sources.label: Yahoo Finance quote/news - SGM.AX (no fresh announcements since Jun 6)
Sims Limited ASX filings page (no new material filing found)
The Bull - Sims shares at fresh highs on FY26 profit optimism (momentum context)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new ASX filing, contract or guidance since Jun 6; today's +2.2% is low-magnitude no-news drift to fresh multi-year highs, and yesterday's hold already reconciled the ~+51% YTD run, so re-penalizing the same rerating two days running isn't warranted. Shave one point for continued momentum-driven multiple expansion that slowly erodes the 'latent' character on a strict screen.
verdicts.bull: Sims Lifecycle Services is a real, under-modeled lever on AI buildout: data-center decommissioning/ITAD volumes scale with hardware refresh cycles, and strong scrap markets plus FY26 profit expectations give earnings support beneath the narrative.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis intact but increasingly recognized: +54% YTD and fresh 12-month-plus highs with no incremental fundamental datapoint since the last result means the easy mispricing has partly closed; rank 41 on this page still prices it as a peripheral node, which looks about right.
verdicts.bear: The AI-circularity angle is a small slice of a cyclical scrap-metal earnings base; a turn in ferrous/non-ferrous spreads or US scrap volumes would unwind the rerating quickly, and at multi-year highs the entry asymmetry has deteriorated.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY26 full-year result / guidance (Aug 2026) quantifying Sims Lifecycle Services growth from data-center decommissioning, or a named hyperscaler ITAD contract.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding into the circularity narrative reverses on weaker scrap prices or soft US volumes, compressing the multiple after a +54% YTD run with no new evidence.
